title: HAProxy HTTP Compression Ratio Effectiveness
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder, Splunk Connect for Syslog, or HTTP Event Collector; `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for listed sourcetypes
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="haproxy:http"
| eval cin=tonumber(comp_in), cout=tonumber(comp_out)
| eval ratio=if(cin>0, round(100*cout/cin,1), null())
| timechart span=1h avg(ratio) by frontend
description: Compression reduces egress cost until misconfiguration disables it for large JSON.
value: Quantifies CDN and origin offload ROI.
implementation: Add compression byte fields per HAProxy docs; document field order for parsers.
visualization: Time series for rates and latency, top/dedup for hotspots, single-value alerts on health thresholds.
grandmaExplanation: We watch haproxy http compression ratio effectiveness and catch issues early, before they turn into outages for the people who rely on the network.
knownFalsePositives: Edge cases: multi-vendor mixes and ad hoc feeds can include lab traffic and partial visibility that mimics issues until scopes are defined. Tuning tip: match this to «HAProxy HTTP Compression Ratio Effectiveness» and exclude change windows, scans, and lab VLANs you already expect.
